title: Inspect & Measure: dimension/measure tool, edge-map smart snapping, alignment guides
goal: The user's PRIMARY workflow: screenshotting UX and redlining it — measuring gaps/sizes, checking alignment, calling out spacing inconsistencies. Build a designer's redline kit on the existing non-destructive annotation model: a measure/dimension tool whose pixel sizes are auto-labeled (toggleable, restyleable, per-layer), plus SMART SNAPPING that magnetizes measure endpoints and alignment guides to UI-element edges detected in the screenshot bitmap (there is NO UI tree — snapping comes from image edge analysis). Auto-inconsistency detection is a timeboxed spike after the core lands. Sequenced AHEAD of phases 14/15 because it is the main use case.

decisions.units: REVISED 2026-06-28 (user): readouts default to PIXELS (raw image px), with a per-measure pt|px toggle (pixels FIRST in the control). `points` divides raw px by `PhotonzDocument.pixelScale` (default 1; ≤0→1 guard). Document coords == image pixels (`canvasSize = ref.pixelSize`, Document.swift:23). The 1×/2× image-scale inspector control was REMOVED as confusing; `pixelScale` plumbing stays for a future AUTO-DETECT from the capture's DPI/backingScaleFactor (NOT yet wired — so points==pixels at the default scale of 1 until auto-detect lands). Stroke width is in LOGICAL pixels (×pixelScale at render) so a 1px sizer line aligns to the pixel grid.
decisions.snapping: There is no semantic UI tree — a screenshot is pixels. 'Snap to the edge of a UI element' is delivered via IMAGE EDGE ANALYSIS: a one-time-per-image edge map (CIEdges/Sobel in PhotonzRender) projected onto X and Y axes; the projection PEAKS are the strong vertical/horizontal boundaries and become snap candidates. For clean UX screenshots the contrast edges ARE the element edges. The existing `Snapping` enum only magnetizes a layer frame to canvas edges/center and is NOT reused for content snapping — new EdgeSnapping consumes the edge map.
decisions.model: Measure is its own LayerContent.measure on the two-endpoint pattern (start/end = box corners). `MeasureForm` {line, bracket}; tool DEFAULTS to BRACKET (a squared 'U' wrapping a gap: drag corner A->opposite B, legs reach in from A, connector spans the gap on the far side, label OUTSIDE). bracketAxis = dominant box axis (tall->vertical, wide->horizontal); inspector has explicit Direction toggle + invert (swap corners). The numeric LABEL is part of the layer (toggleable/restyleable). Select shows a dotted box + handles on ALL 4 box corners (line shows 2 ends); corner drag updates the right start/end components (diagonal-opposite fixed), live label, one undo step. Alignment guides (16.6) are a separate persistent-line layer.
decisions.sequencing: 16.1->16.2->16.3 deliver the measure tool (the anchor). 16.4->16.5 deliver the edge map + smart snapping (the killer combo with measure). 16.6 adds alignment guides. 16.7 is the auto-inspect SPIKE, only after 16.1-16.6 prove the edge map is reliable, with a simpler cross-highlight fallback if full auto-detection is flaky.

tasks.title: Measurement model + dimension geometry (core, TDD)
tasks.notes: DONE (490 tests green, +19). New `Sources/PhotonzCore/Measure.swift`: MeasureMode {free|horizontal|vertical}, MeasureUnit {points|pixels}, MeasureCapStyle {ticks|arrows}, MeasureSegment, MeasureGeometry, MeasureContent (start/end + strokeWidth/colorHex/showLabel/unit/decimals/capStyle), MeasureBuilder (layer/updating/resized/restyled, mirrors AnnotationBuilder). Distance: free=hypot, H/V=abs(dx/dy); displayDistance divides by pixelScale for points (0->1 guard), raw for pixels; label() = '%.<decimals>f <suffix>'. Geometry: free=bare segment; H/V level the dimension line onto the END point's axis and drop a witness line from an off-axis START. NEW LayerContent.measure case threaded through: Layer.measure accessor + measureEndpoint + contains (hit near dim+witness lines) + hasEndpointHandles; AnnotationEditing.allowsFrameResize/resized routing; Document.pixelScale (default 1, custom Decodable for legacy back-compat). DocumentRenderer has a NO-OP .measure stub (returns nil) until 16.2. Tests: MeasureTests.swift (distance/units/geometry/builder/pixelScale round-trip+legacy). NOTE: H/V level onto END point's axis (drag-end = where the dimension line sits) — 16.3 drag UX should honor that.

tasks.title: Dimension rasterizer (render, pixel-tested)
tasks.notes: DONE (496 tests green, +6 render). New Sources/PhotonzRender/MeasureRasterizer.swift draws in layer-local top-left space: witness/extension lines, the dimension line, end caps (ticks = perpendicular serif; arrows = inward filled arrowheads), and a toggleable numeric label on a rounded plate filled with the measure color + white CoreText text (AppKit-free CTFramesetter/CTFrameDraw, mirrors TextRasterizer's flipped-context pattern). DocumentRenderer .measure case now rasterizes (replaced the 16.1 no-op stub) with variant 'scale:<pixelScale>' so the points readout invalidates the cache when the document scale changes. GOTCHA fixed: MeasureBuilder.layer now adopts the real span (content.start/end = from/to) BEFORE computing renderPadding/estimatedLabelSize, else updating()/short measures reserved the wrong label width. Frame reserves estimatedLabelSize (centered on the dimension line) so short labels aren't clipped. Tests: MeasureRenderingTests (line, plate on/off, witness, diagonal, Retina pixelScale) + core label-reservation/frame tests. FOLLOW-UP for 16.3: verify label text orientation visually (pixel test only checks presence).

tasks.title: Measure tool UX (app) — INTERACTIVELY VERIFIED, grew well beyond original scope
tasks.notes: DONE + user-verified (502 tests green). Tool.measure ('ruler', M). Base: CanvasView measureDrag/preview, EditorState.addMeasure, MeasureInspector (InspectorSectionID.measure), EditorView wiring. THEN iterated heavily with the user (commits af34f1c..328bf04): (1) BRACKET FORM is now the default — drag-a-box 'U' wrapping a gap, label outside; MeasureForm{line,bracket}, bracketGeometry/bracketAxis/labelCenter; ⇧ flips axis on create; preview draws the bracket path. (2) SELECT/MOVE/RESIZE: dotted selection box + handles on ALL 4 box corners (MeasureCornerDrag(xFromEnd,yFromEnd); measureCorners()); live label via previewMeasureEndpoints, one undo via commitMeasureEndpoints; move via Layer.contains hitting the drawn strokes; editEndpoint unifies annotation+measure endpoint hit-testing. (3) Inspector: Style(Bracket/Line), Direction(V/H) + invert ⇄ (invertMeasure swaps corners), Unit(Pixels FIRST/Points), Thickness(1/2/3px), Show-label, Color; image-scale control REMOVED. (4) Stroke width = LOGICAL px (×pixelScale, butt caps+miter joins) default 1; unit default PIXELS. (5) Label orientation bug FIXED (blit TextRasterizer glyphs, don't draw CoreText into the flipped ctx) + orientation guard test. (6) Universal selection outline (systemBlue, 2px, dotted, 60%) for ALL object types, HIDDEN during any resize. (7) SEPARATE BUG FIXED + verified: region capture overlay no longer activates the app / raises the editor (non-activating panels, see capture.md). REMAINING FOLLOW-UPS: measureStyle not persisted; pixelScale auto-detect from capture DPI not wired; toolbar S popover doesn't cover measures.

tasks.title: Edge-map analysis (render + core, TDD)
tasks.notes: Build a per-image edge map from the base image: gradient/edge pass (CIEdges or Sobel via CIConvolution) in PhotonzRender, then PROJECT magnitude onto X and Y -> 1-D profiles whose thresholded peaks are the strong vertical (X) and horizontal (Y) boundaries. Core owns the peak-finding + EdgeMap model (sorted candidate x's and y's with strengths); render owns the CI pass that produces the projection buffers. Cache per ImageRef. TESTS FIRST (core, synthetic): white rect on black -> detected x-edges at rect L/R, y-edges at T/B; noise below threshold rejected; nearby peaks deduped.

tasks.title: Ruler snapping: corner grips magnetize to detected edges + pixel grid (core + app) — USER'S NEXT ASK
tasks.notes: This is what the user calls 'ruler snapping' (their 3rd measure request). New EdgeSnapping (core): given a dragged point, the EdgeMap, zoom, and a screen tolerance (~6pt), snap X to the nearest in-tolerance vertical edge and Y to the nearest horizontal edge; ALSO snap to the integer PIXEL GRID (round to image px) so thin 1px sizer lines land crisp (the user explicitly wants pixel-perfect alignment — see the 16.3 logical-px stroke work). Return snapped point + which guide(s) captured (for a highlight overlay). App: wire into the MeasureCornerDrag in CanvasView (mouseDragged computes the dragged corner, snaps it before previewMeasureEndpoints), render a highlight on the snapped edge while held. The user's workflow: rough-in a bracket, then drag the misaligned top/bottom (or any) corner and have it click onto the real UI edge. TESTS FIRST: snaps to nearest peak within tolerance, no-op beyond tolerance, picks nearest of two, snaps to pixel grid, tolerance scales with zoom. NOTE: snapping applies per-axis independently (a corner can snap X to an edge while Y free).

tasks.title: Alignment guides: draggable snapping rulers (core + app)
tasks.notes: Persistent H/V guide lines dropped on the canvas, draggable, snapping to edges via 16.5; toggleable + configurable (color, on/off) as layers. Serves 'draw lines on the edges of text to check alignment.' Core: guide model + axis position clamp to canvas. App: drop/drag/delete, ruler affordance, render. Mostly app; keep position/clamp math in core with a couple of tests.

tasks.title: Auto-inspect spike: flag spacing inconsistencies (after 16.1-16.6)
tasks.notes: TIMEBOXED EXPLORATION, only once the edge map proves reliable. From the EdgeMap, find the dominant bands/columns, measure gaps between consecutive edges, take the mode, and FLAG outliers (16,16,16,17 -> highlight the 17 + delta). Scope via marquee. If full auto-detection is flaky, ship the simpler fallback: a row of gap-measures that cross-highlight mismatched values. Explicitly research-y; capture findings in notes regardless of how far it gets.
